authority: Official Pukas model descriptions and governed Quivrr catalogue records

models.reason: Pukas and Christenson position Pegaso as a performance-based twin that retains fish glide and drive while supporting a more vertical approach.
The model is explicitly designed as an easy, versatile longboard across varied conditions, with trim, movement and manoeuvrability as its core role.
Beachy Mood is expressly positioned as a daily surf companion for easy, versatile performance in small to medium beach breaks.
Its step-down sizing, generous nose, wide tail, lazy rocker and softer-wave focus make weak-wave speed and ease the primary design role.
This is explicitly an old-school fish adapted for modern pocket surfing, retaining fish identity while adding rocker, lower rails and vertical capability.
Flying Diamond is an easy-riding, performance-oriented twin with broad travel and beach-break range rather than a fish-first design identity.
The Biolos collaboration is a racey hybrid-performance board for European waves, balancing paddle support, speed and responsiveness across everyday conditions.
Magnetic is explicitly a medium-to-long twin-fin platform combining glide, flow and modern performance for moderate to powerful waves.
Mustache is a new-school fish concept using twin or two-plus-one setups, fish speed and a winged swallow while adding stronger vertical control.
Pukas describes Performance Mood as a high-performance surfboard for vertical lines, rail surfing, speed and modern manoeuvres, not a general hybrid fallback.
Space Oddity is explicitly presented as a one-board quiver combining paddle power, ease, broad range and high-performance control.
Water Lion is designed for solid European surf and sits deliberately between a performance shortboard and step-up, with hollow-wave control defining the primary role.
The Ultra is the sharper pro-level extension of Water Lion for skilled surfers in serious waves, prioritising refined high-performance response over general step-up paddle support.

review_notes: All 13 governed Pukas models have an explicit public family and detailed category.
Easy Mood was moved out of the broad Daily Driver fallback because softer-wave ease and step-down performance are its primary role.
Performance Mood was moved to Performance Shortboard because Pukas explicitly defines it through high-performance surfing intent.
Water Lion remains Step Up, while Water Lion Ultra is treated as the sharper good-wave performance shortboard variant.
Twin-fin configuration alone does not create Fish classification; manufacturer intent and overall design role remain authoritative.
